candidates.snippet:
runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: voidzero-dev/setup-vp@v1
        with:

findings.title: Unpinned third-party action and no least-privilege permissions block
findings.description: The CI job pins actions to mutable tags rather than commit SHAs: `actions/checkout@v4` (L13) and the third-party `voidzero-dev/setup-vp@v1` (L14). A mutable tag can be re-pointed by a compromised maintainer to attacker code that then executes in CI; the third-party `voidzero-dev/setup-vp@v1` is the higher-risk one (first-party `actions/checkout` is lower risk). The workflow also declares no top-level `permissions:` block, so the GITHUB_TOKEN falls back to the repository default, which can be read-write depending on org/repo settings. The workflow triggers on `pull_request` and `push`; for fork PRs the token is read-only by default and no secrets are referenced, which limits blast radius — hence medium/low severity rather than high.
findings.recommendation: Pin actions to full commit SHAs (e.g. `voidzero-dev/setup-vp@<sha> # v1`) and add an explicit least-privilege `permissions: { contents: read }` block at the top of the workflow.
